heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions Across the North West
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions North West | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for North West homes, made in our Blackburn factory and installed on site in under a week by EazyBase.
intro:
The North West is where EazyBase began, and it's still the heart of everything we do. Every extension we build starts life in our Blackburn factory, which means homeowners here enjoy something no one else in the country gets: we're quite literally on your doorstep. Deliveries are shorter, scheduling is simpler, and our installation teams know the region's streets and housing stock inside out.

If you've been weighing up a traditional build — months of scaffolding, skips on the drive and trades coming and going — there's a calmer way to do it. We design and factory-build modular home extensions in North West towns and cities in as little as four weeks, then install on site in under a week. A new kitchen, dining space, home office, playroom or garden room, without your home becoming a building site.

From Victorian terraces in the mill towns to inter-war and post-war semis across Greater Manchester, Lancashire, Merseyside and Cheshire, we've shaped our approach around the homes people here actually live in. Explore our local town pages below, or get in touch and we'll talk through what's possible on your plot.

localAngles.heading: Our home region — and our fastest installs
localAngles.body: Because every EazyBase extension is manufactured in Blackburn, North West projects benefit from the shortest journey between factory and front door. That closeness matters more than you might think. It means flexible delivery slots, installation teams who aren't racing a long-distance schedule, and a simpler path back to us for surveys, design visits and aftercare. While we install across the UK, homes in the North West sit closest to the people who design, build and fit your extension — so if anything ever needs attention, we're nearby, not at the other end of a motorway.

localAngles.heading: Extending North West terraces and semis
localAngles.body: The region's housing stock is wonderfully consistent: brick terraces from the industrial era, generous 1930s semis, and post-war family homes with usable rear gardens. These properties suit modular extension beautifully. A factory-built rear addition can turn a narrow galley kitchen into a proper kitchen-diner, or give a semi the open family space the original floor plan never allowed. Because the structure arrives largely complete, we spend days on your property rather than months — a real advantage on tighter terraced plots where a long traditional build disrupts the whole street.

localAngles.heading: Planning permission in the North West
localAngles.body: Planning here works the same way it does across England: permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions within set size limits, meaning many projects don't need a full planning application at all. That said, rules differ if your home is in a conservation area, is a listed building, or has had its permitted development rights removed — always check with your local council before committing. As part of every EazyBase project we review the planning position with you at the design stage, so you know exactly where you stand before anything is built.

localAngles.heading: Where we work across the region
localAngles.body: From our Blackburn base we cover the whole North West — Lancashire's towns, Greater Manchester, Merseyside, Cheshire and the Fylde coast. Each area has its own character, from stone-built terraces at the edge of the Pennines to red-brick suburbs and coastal semis, and our designs flex to match. We've created dedicated local pages for the towns we serve most often, covering the housing styles, access considerations and typical projects we see in each. Choose your town below, or contact us directly if you're anywhere in between — chances are we're already working nearby.

faqs.q: How quickly can you build and install an extension in the North West?
faqs.a: Faster here than anywhere. Your extension is factory-built in Blackburn in as little as four weeks, and because North West homes are so close to the factory, delivery and installation are easy to schedule. Once the module arrives, on-site installation typically takes under a week.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission for a modular extension?
faqs.a: Often not. Permitted development rights frequently cover single-storey rear extensions within certain size limits, so many North West projects proceed without a full planning application. Conservation areas, listed buildings and homes with restricted rights are exceptions — always check with your local council, and we'll help you review the position during design.

faqs.q: My house is a terrace with limited access — can you still install?
faqs.a: Usually, yes. Tight rear access is common across the North West's terraced streets, and it's something we assess at the survey stage. Depending on the plot we can adjust module sizes, plan crane placement or arrange delivery logistics around parking and street access before anything is built.

faqs.q: What do North West homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: Kitchen-diners are the most popular by far — opening up the back of a terrace or semi transforms how the whole house works. Home offices, kids' playrooms, dining rooms and stand-alone garden rooms are close behind, and many projects combine two uses in one well-designed space.
